summary:
The type IV isthmus as described by Kim and colleagues involves connections among at least three root canals that may be either completely formed or partially open; it does not align with simple binary choices presented like canal fusion versus separation alone. To accurately reflect their findings would require specifying these nuances regarding multiple interconnected pathways within one tooth structure. ```plaintext
The type IV isthmus according to Kim and colleagues connects at least three root canals, which might be fully developed or only partly closed; this contrasts sharply with simpler classifications such as mere canal union or division.
```
